pairs.incomplete_question: The figure below shows a grid of [BLANK] squares in a row. Each square has a diagonal connecting its lower left vertex to its upper right vertex. A bug moves along the line segments from vertex to vertex, never traversing the same segment twice and never moving from right to left along a horizontal or diagonal segment. Let $N$ be the number of paths the bug can take from the lower left corner ($A$) to the upper right corner ($B$). One such path from $A$ to $B$ is shown by the thick line segments in the figure. Find \sqrt{N}.
